content.problemStatement:
Election season has started in Chefland and the election commission wants to know the count of eligible voters.

There are NNN people in Chefland where the age of the ithi^{th}ith person in AiA_iAi​.

Given that a person needs to be **at least** XXX years old to vote, find the number of eligible voters.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of people in Chefland, and the minimum age required for a person to vote in Chefland.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the age of the ithi^{th}ith person.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of eligible voters in Chefland.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line of a test case contains two integers N and X — the number of people and the minimum voting age. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN, the ages of the people.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of people whose age is greater than or equal to X.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: Minimum age = 3, ages >=3 are [5,3] → 2 voters.
Test case 2: Minimum age = 2, ages >=2 are [3,4] → 2 voters.
Test case 3: Minimum age = 2, ages >=2 are [2,2,4] → 3 voters.
Test case 4: Minimum age = 6, no age reaches 6 → 0 voters.
All five people are exactly the minimum voting age, so all are eligible.
No one is at least 10 years old, so zero eligible voters.

solution.approach: For each test case we read N and X, then iterate over the N ages and increment a counter whenever the current age is >= X. After processing all ages we output the counter. This is O(N) time per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) where N is the number of people in a test case (at most 100).
